{{Infobox group
| name = D’Niss
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[D’Niss Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The D’Niss are a highly intelligent and sapient race of massive, psychic insects. They function as a coordinated swarm that serves their ruler, the [[Swarm-Queen]] [[Esh’kinaar]], whom they worship as a deity. The species is characterized by its predatory nature; while their soldiers act as armies of locusts, the larger D’Niss are titanic creatures capable of consuming entire stars. Unlike other threats that may target any location, the D’Niss specifically seek out and feed on civilized, developed worlds rather than barren ones. In combat, the D’Niss are formidable opponents who utilize advanced magic and can execute subspace blockades to isolate systems. Their magical symbols resemble insect tracks rather than humanoid script, and their spells have shown the ability to evolve and adapt independently. Although a group known as the [[Titan Knights]] previously drove back the swarm, the threat of a D’Niss incursion remains a significant concern for the galaxy. They have previously caused massive devastation, including the destruction of entire systems and the invasion of planets like [[Visiria]].}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The D’Niss are titanic, bug-like insects associated with the [[Swarm-Queen]] [[Esh’kinaar]]. These creatures possess the terrifying capability to consume an entire planet. Although their queen is currently under control and not on the verge of returning, the D’Niss remain a primary target for extermination.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[D’Niss]] are titanic insects whose queen is currently under control.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5">''The Captain'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[D’Niss]] are described as bugs.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[D’Niss]] are a target for extermination.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[D’Niss]] are associated with the [[Swarm-Queen]] [[Esh’kinaar]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A [[D’Niss]] is a creature capable of eating a planet.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22">''The Captain'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The D'Niss [[Swarm-Queen]] is not currently on the brink of returning.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The D’Niss are a group that [[Starhammer]] once fought against during his era of conquest, an era in which he was capable of tearing through a full-grown member of their kind. Although they are not currently pushing into the [[Captain]]'s dimension, their potential return remains a constant possibility. Because they could reappear at any time, [[Raion]] intends to defend against their eventual homecoming.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[D’Niss]] are a group whose return [[Raion]] intends to defend against.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c5">''The Engineer'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[D’Niss]] was one of the groups [[Starhammer]] fought during his conquest.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c7">''The Engineer'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[D’Niss]] are not currently pushing into the [[Captain]]'s dimension.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] tore through a full-grown [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39">''The Engineer'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The [[D’Niss]] could return at any time.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46">''The Engineer'', chapter 46</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The D’Niss are insectoid, multi-headed space monsters that serve the [[Swarm-Queen]] and possess magical abilities, including the capacity to channel [[Aether]] on a massive scale. Historically, these straightforward villains attacked three [[Iron Hives]] and were fought by the [[Knights]], leading to the formation of the [[Titan Knights]]. During their recent return, these winged entities have conducted incursions on the edge of [[Dark Space]] and burrowed into every moon and planet within the [[Visiri system]] to feed. Utilizing psychic assaults, subspace lurking, and shadow-based vanishing, the D’Niss army—which includes tougher locust soldiers—has faced intense combat from the [[Karoshan]] fleets, [[King]] [[Felrex]], and the [[Red]] [[Titan]]. The group is further linked to [[Esh’kinaar]], who uses spawn magic to create these creatures and may be laying eggs within a star.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are an alien incursion that has been increasing their activity.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c2">''The Knight'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The D'Niss are an enemy group that the [[Knights]] fought.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c7">''The Knight'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] attacked three [[Iron Hives]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9">''The Knight'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The war against the [[D’Niss]] was over years before the narrator's ritual.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c10">''The Knight'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are giant space monsters that the [[Knights]] defend against.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12">''The Knight'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] possess magic.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The D'Niss are a group that could potentially be left to the galaxy.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c13">''The Knight'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are described as straightforward villains.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c15">''The Knight'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are returning.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17">''The Knight'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The D'Niss are a group of entities.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Shyrax's ships resemble a swarm of D'Niss soldiers.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18">''The Knight'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are conducting an incursion on the edge of [[Dark Space]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c19">''The Knight'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Titan Knights]] were formed to fight the [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20">''The Knight'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] is a group or entity with an unbroken shell.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A blue lance of water sliced through the [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are an entity that requires a secure staging ground against them.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23">''The Knight'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Refugees from nearby worlds are fleeing the [[D’Niss]] advance.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] feed on planets.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are the enemies of the [[Titan Force]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c31">''The Knight'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are a group that the [[Karoshan]] fleets are targeting.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c33">''The Knight'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] exist to serve the [[Swarm-Queen]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c34">''The Knight'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are insectoid soldiers.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35">''The Knight'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[D’Niss soldiers]] use psychic assault to soften up opponents.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Perfected]] are capable of taking down a [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[D’Niss]] are capable of lurking in [[Subspace]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c35" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The D'Niss are capable of channeling [[Aether]] at massive scales.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37">''The Knight'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A magical battle was occurring between the [[D’Niss]] and [[King]] [[Felrex]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are an enemy group gathering forces and armies.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38">''The Knight'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are planning an incursion.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c38" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[D’Niss]] are insects that burrow into every moon and planet in the [[Visiri system]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are located in a system.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are an ant-like entity with multiple heads.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] is a summoning spell.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40">''The Knight'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are a group that [[Esh’kinaar]] belongs to.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Esh’kinaar]] might be laying eggs of a future [[D’Niss]] into the star.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Raion]] is fighting the last of the [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c41">''The Knight'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The locust soldiers are tougher than standard [[D’Niss soldiers]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The D�Niss army is being fought by the [[Red]] [[Titan]].}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] were involved in a battle involving [[Esh’kinaar]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Millions of [[D’Niss soldiers]] have been killed.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c43">''The Knight'', chapter 43</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are creatures that can be created via [[Esh’kinaar]]'s spawn magic.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c44">''The Knight'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] are capable of vanishing in a flurry of shadow.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c44" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[D’Niss]] has wings and upper claws.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c44" />}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Described as giant bugs resembling armies of locusts, the D’Niss are rare and magically valuable creatures that spawn smaller offspring. During an incursion on [[Visiria]], these beings devastated the system and caused significant losses to the [[Nova-Bots]]. [[The Last Horizon]] has faced this group, which includes entities capable of delivering [[sword]]-strikes that stretch between planets. Although the [[Captain]] ruined a mech in combat against them, the D’Niss currently exist as a scattered, defeated, or imprisoned group outside of this universe. Despite their current state, they are known for the ability to resurface and return even stronger.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] are creatures that spawn smaller offspring.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c1">''The Pilot'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] conducted an incursion on [[Visiria]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2">''The Pilot'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] are a group that is scattered, defeated, dead, or locked away from this universe.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|A [[sword]]-strike on the level of the [[D’Niss]] can stretch between planets.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c12">''The Pilot'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The D'Niss are rare and magically valuable.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The D'Niss is a group that the [[Captain]] ruined a mech against.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] are described as giant bugs that can pop up again and come back stronger.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Last Horizon]] has faced the [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] incursion devastated the [[Visiria system]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c27">''The Pilot'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] are described as armies of locusts.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c27" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]] adapted psychic scanning from the [[D’Niss]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[D’Niss]] caused losses to the [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c46">''The Pilot'', chapter 46</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“I saw him tear through a full-grown D’Niss,” I said, I mean, flew straight through.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“I think three Iron Hives were attacked by the D’Niss.”<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c9" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“The D’Niss are coming back,” I said to Raion.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon groups]]
